text.title: api-graphql Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-graphql as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Resolver that eagerly loads all relationships on the parent type, regardless of whether the client requested them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always-loaded relations execute queries even when the field is not selected, wasting DB resources on every request.

failure-mode.description: List field that returns all items without pagination: async def users(self) -> List[User].
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Could return millions of rows in a single request, causing full table scans and OOM errors. All list fields that could grow must use cursor-based pagination.

failure-mode.description: Adding a new sensitive field (email, apiKey, passwordHash) to a type without field-level authorization checks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any client that can query the parent type can retrieve the sensitive field. Auth audits should be automated via a schema lint rule.

failure-mode.description: Leaving GraphQL introspection enabled in production without authentication.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Introspection exposes the full schema to attackers, enabling targeted injection and DoS attacks. Gate it behind admin auth or disable it entirely.
